title: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup

seo.title: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup: Easy Indian Dessert Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making fragrant Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup at home! This easy, authentic Indian dessert recipe is perfect for any occasion. Get step-by-step guide.

easy Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup recipe

seo.h1: Authentic Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup Recipe
h2Headings: What is Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup?
Ingredients for Homemade Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup
seo.imageAlt: A close-up of soft, rose-infused Gulab Jamun, garnished with pistachios and dried rose petals, on a white plate.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the fragrant sweetness of homemade Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup, a classic Indian dessert perfect for special occasions.

description: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup is a classic Indian sweet featuring deep-fried milk solids dumplings soaked in a fragrant rose-infused sugar syrup. These soft, melt-in-your-mouth spheres offer a delightful balance of sweetness and aromatic floral notes. It is a cherished dessert, perfect for celebrations and a comforting treat any time.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gulab Jamun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine grated khoa, all-purpose flour, and baking soda. Knead gently to form a smooth, soft dough. Avoid over-kneading, as this can make the jamuns hard. If the dough feels too dry, add a tiny bit of milk, a few drops at a time, until it comes together.

instructions.title: Form the Jamuns
instructions.text: Divide the dough into small, equal portions. Roll each portion between your palms to form smooth, crack-free balls. Ensure there are no cracks, as these can cause the jamuns to break during frying. Set aside on a plate.

instructions.title: Make the Rose Sugar Syrup
instructions.text: In a wide, heavy-bottomed pan, combine sugar and water. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves completely. Add the crushed cardamom pods and saffron strands if using. Simmer for about 5-7 minutes until the syrup is slightly sticky but not too thick (no specific string consistency needed). Remove from heat and stir in the rose water. Keep the syrup warm.

instructions.title: Heat Ghee for Frying
instructions.text: Heat ghee in a deep frying pan or kadai over low-medium heat. To test if the ghee is ready, drop a tiny piece of dough; it should slowly rise to the surface without browning too quickly. The ghee should be warm, not smoking hot.

instructions.title: Fry the Gulab Jamuns
instructions.text: Gently slide the prepared jamun balls into the warm ghee, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan. Fry on low-medium heat, continuously stirring and turning them gently with a slotted spoon, until they turn an even golden brown or dark reddish-brown color from all sides. This slow frying ensures they cook through evenly.

instructions.title: Soak in Syrup
instructions.text: Once fried, immediately remove the hot gulab jamuns from the ghee using a slotted spoon and transfer them directly into the warm rose sugar syrup. Ensure they are fully submerged. Let them soak for at least 30 minutes, or preferably for 2-3 hours, to absorb the syrup and become soft and spongy.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the gulab jamuns have absorbed the syrup and plumped up, transfer them to a serving bowl. Garnish with chopped pistachios and dried rose petals. Serve warm or at room temperature.

tips: For perfectly soft gulab jamuns, knead the dough gently and ensure there are no cracks in the balls before frying. Over-kneading or cracks will result in hard jamuns.
Fry the gulab jamuns on very low to medium heat. If the ghee is too hot, the jamuns will brown quickly on the outside and remain raw inside.
Always soak the hot fried jamuns directly into warm (not boiling or cold) syrup. This allows them to absorb the syrup properly and become juicy and soft.
article: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup is a beloved Indian Desserts dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
